source: SEC EDGAR (real companyfacts XBRL, live-fetched via data/edgar_fundamentals.py)
generated_note: Real PIT fundamentals for the tests/test_validation_multifactor.py 10-ticker universe (2009-2026, bounded by SEC XBRL mandate start ~2009 and this repo 's current date). report_date = the SEC filing date each fact became public (the PIT key). Prices were reconstructed to their TRUE nominal historical value at each report_date by un-adjusting yfinance's retroactively split-adjusted Close using real split-history data (yf.Ticker.splits) -- EDGAR EPS/shares figures are as-filed and never retroactively restated for later stock splits, so combining them with a still-split-adjusted price would silently distort market_cap/pe_ratio/pb_ratio for any ticker that later split (AAPL 7:1 2014 + 4:1 2020, GE reverse+forward splits, KO 2:1 2012, etc). XOM has only 1 row: SEC currently maps ticker XOM to a newly-reorganized holding entity (CIK 2115436) with minimal own filing history -- a genuine real-world data artifact, not a bug.
